name: Mistral Large 3
description: Mistral AI's open-weight flagship released December 2025 under Apache 2.0: a sparse MoE (675B total / 41B active) multimodal model with ~256K context and 40+ languages. Debuted #2 among open-source non-reasoning models on LMArena, with a strong EU data-sovereignty story.

evidence.value: Strong coding performance reported across standard benchmarks for an open-weight model

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Review of provider benchmarks and community evaluations of open weights

evidence.value: Competitive math and reasoning results among non-reasoning (single-pass) models

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Review of reasoning benchmarks; model is non-reasoning class (no extended thinking)

evidence.value: Debuted #2 among open-source non-reasoning models on LMArena
State-of-the-art open-weight performance across knowledge and multilingual tasks

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Crowdsourced arena comparisons and provider benchmark suite

evidence.value: Stable instruction following reported across hosted and self-hosted deployments

output_consistency.methodology: Community repeated-prompt testing on open weights

evidence.value: 41B active parameters keep inference fast for the model's scale

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency from third-party benchmarking of hosted endpoints

evidence.value: Tail latency varies by host (Mistral, Bedrock, Azure, self-hosted)
Approximately 256K token context window
Stable hosted-API availability; self-hosting removes provider dependency entirely

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime of hosted API; open weights enable customer-controlled availability

performance_reliability.notes: Best-in-class open-weight performance for its release window: sparse MoE (675B total / 41B active) delivers near-frontier quality with modest active compute. Non-reasoning class — frontier reasoning models outperform it on hard multi-step problems.

evidence.value: Instruction-hierarchy training; limited published red-team results

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection patterns

evidence.value: Reasonable default refusals; open weights allow guardrail removal by deployers

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing on hosted and open-weight deployments
jailbreak_resistance.notes: Open weights mean downstream deployments can weaken or strengthen safety behavior

evidence.value: EU-based provider under GDPR; self-hosting keeps data entirely in customer infrastructure

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies plus self-hosting option

evidence.value: Optional moderation API and system-prompt guardrailing available

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; cloud-provider controls on Bedrock/Azure

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features across hosting options

security.notes: Solid security with the open-weights caveat: deployers control (and can remove) guardrails, so deployment-level controls matter more than for closed models.

data_residency.value: EU (hosted API); anywhere via self-hosting or cloud region choice

evidence.value: EU provider with European hosting; open weights allow full on-premises deployment

data_residency.methodology: Review of hosting documentation and deployment options

evidence.value: API data not used for training by default for paid tiers; self-hosting eliminates the question entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of terms of service and data usage policy

data_retention.value: Limited retention on hosted API; zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: Short-term retention for abuse monitoring on La Plateforme; customer-controlled when self-hosted

data_retention.methodology: Review of retention policies across deployment modes

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; self-hosting keeps PII in-house
EU provider natively under GDPR; SOC 2 for hosted platform; Bedrock/Azure hosting inherits those clouds' certifications

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of certifications across Mistral and cloud hosting partners

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 weights enable fully air-gapped, zero-external-retention deployments

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of self-hosting options enabling complete data control

privacy_compliance.notes: Standout data-sovereignty story: EU provider under GDPR, plus Apache 2.0 weights allow fully on-premises/air-gapped deployment — the strongest possible residency guarantee.

evidence.value: Clear step-by-step explanations; open weights permit deep inspection and research

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency; open weights enable interpretability research

evidence.value: Typical hallucination rates for its class; no built-in grounding

hallucination_rate.methodology: Factual QA testing by community evaluators

evidence.value: Multilingual training (40+ languages) reduces anglocentric bias; limited published bias evaluation

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of bias disclosures and multilingual evaluation

evidence.value: Adequate uncertainty expression; raw logprobs accessible via open weights

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment plus open-weight logprob access

evidence.value: Public model card with architecture details (675B MoE / 41B active), license, and usage guidance

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of published model card and architecture disclosure

evidence.value: Architecture fully disclosed; training data composition described only at a high level

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Optional moderation and system-prompt guardrails; defaults lighter than closed flagships and removable by deployers

trust_transparency.notes: Open weights provide architectural transparency rare at this scale (675B MoE disclosed), though training data detail and built-in guardrails are lighter than closed frontier models.

evidence.value: Clean OpenAI-compatible API with streaming, function calling, JSON mode, and vision

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official Python and TypeScript SDKs; first-class vLLM and transformers support for self-hosting

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK and inference-stack support

evidence.value: Dated model versions with deprecation notices; open weights never disappear once downloaded

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy; open weights eliminate forced-retirement risk

evidence.value: Usage dashboard on hosted platform; self-hosted observability is customer-built

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of monitoring tools across deployment modes

evidence.value: Good documentation, enterprise support contracts, active community

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation and support channels

evidence.value: Available on Hugging Face, Amazon Bedrock, Azure, and Mistral's La Plateforme; broad vLLM/community tooling

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of distribution channels and third-party tooling

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 open weights: unrestricted commercial use, modification, and redistribution

operational_excellence.notes: Apache 2.0 licensing at frontier scale is the headline: no usage restrictions, no vendor lock-in, and availability across HF, Bedrock, Azure, and La Plateforme.

code-generation.notes: Strong open-weight coding; closed frontier flagships still lead on hard software engineering.

customer-support.notes: 40+ languages, low active-parameter inference cost, and self-hosting make it excellent for global support.

content-creation.notes: Strong multilingual content generation; particularly good for European-language work.

data-analysis.notes: Capable analysis within 256K context; lacks extended-reasoning mode for hardest problems.

research-assistant.notes: Good synthesis over long documents; self-hosting suits sensitive research corpora.

legal-compliance.notes: EU provider under GDPR plus on-premises deployment is compelling for European legal workloads.

healthcare.notes: Self-hosting keeps PHI fully in-house, sidestepping vendor BAA questions; validate clinical accuracy.

financial-analysis.notes: Solid quantitative work; data-sovereign deployment appeals to EU financial institutions.

education.notes: Multilingual strength and low cost suit global education deployments.

creative-writing.notes: Good multilingual creative range; less distinctive than closed frontier flagships.

strengths: Apache 2.0 open weights at frontier scale — full commercial freedom and no lock-in
Sparse MoE efficiency: 675B total but only 41B active parameters per token
Debuted #2 among open-source non-reasoning models on LMArena
EU provider with strong GDPR/data-sovereignty posture; fully self-hostable
Multimodal (text + image) with 40+ languages and ~256K context
Broad availability: Hugging Face, Amazon Bedrock, Azure, La Plateforme
limitations: Non-reasoning class — trails frontier reasoning models on hard multi-step problems
Self-hosting 675B weights requires substantial GPU infrastructure despite 41B active
Hosted API pricing ($2/$6 per 1M on La Plateforme) is several times higher than early aggregator estimates suggested, though output remains cheaper than closed flagships
Open weights let deployers strip guardrails, shifting safety burden downstream
Training data composition only described at a high level
best_for: Data-sovereign deployments requiring on-premises or EU-resident inference
Organizations wanting frontier-class quality without vendor lock-in
Cost-efficient high-volume inference leveraging sparse MoE economics
not_recommended_for: Hardest reasoning workloads better served by extended-thinking models
Teams without MLOps capacity attempting self-hosted deployment at this scale
Applications relying on strong provider-enforced guardrails by default

pricing.notes: Official La Plateforme pricing per mistral.ai/pricing (verified 2026-07-09); corrects earlier aggregator-based estimate of ~$0.50/$1.50. Batch processing gets a 50% discount; cloud-host (Bedrock/Azure) rates vary. Self-hosting under Apache 2.0 incurs only infrastructure cost.

metadata.architecture: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts transformer: 675B total parameters, 41B active per token
